pattern: Every diagonal is its own independent path through the grid, so the two open-ended walks from a cell -- one heading toward the top-left corner, one toward the bottom-right corner -- never overlap and can be counted separately with a plain set.
hints: For a cell (r, c), the top-left diagonal is exactly the cells (r-1,c-1), (r-2,c-2), ... and the bottom-right diagonal is (r+1,c+1), (r+2,c+2), ... -- neither walk ever touches the other or the starting cell itself.
You only need the number of DISTINCT values seen on each of those two walks, not their sum or the full list -- a set built while walking outward works fine given how small the grid is.

The answer at each cell is the absolute value of (distinct count on the top-left walk) minus (distinct count on the bottom-right walk); corner and edge cells simply have one or both walks empty, which contributes a count of zero, not an error.

edge_inputs.description: typical mixed 3x3 grid with several repeated values
single cell has no diagonal neighbors in either direction
single row -- there is no vertical movement, so every diagonal walk is empty
single column -- symmetric case, every diagonal walk is empty
every value identical, so both diagonal distinct counts never exceed one and most differences are zero
minimum and maximum allowed cell values placed together

stress.description: random rectangular grids of varying shape and value spread
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, min(size, 20))
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 20))
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def differenceOfDistinctValues(self, grid):
        m = len(grid)
        n = len(grid[0])
        ans = [[0] * n for _ in range(m)]
        for r in range(m):
            for c in range(n):
                tl = set()
                rr, cc = r - 1, c - 1
                while rr >= 0 and cc >= 0:
                    tl.add(grid[rr][cc])
                    rr -= 1
                    cc -= 1
                br = set()
                rr, cc = r + 1, c + 1
                while rr < m and cc < n:
                    br.add(grid[rr][cc])
                    rr += 1
                    cc += 1
                ans[r][c] = abs(len(tl) - len(br))
        return ans
